beans.name: 에티오피아 시다마 트와콕 내추럴 100g
beans.description:
복숭아 또는 플로럴 뉘앙스나 무게감 있는 커피를 찾으신다면 추천드려요
Sidama Region
2022 뉴크롭
Heirloom
Natural
트와콕은 에티오피아어로 Sweet peach 라는 의미입니다.
2018년에 트와콕 셀렉션이 처음 런칭된 후로 매년 빠른 시간에 완판을 기록하며 많은 사랑을 받고 있습니다.
잘 익은 빨간 체리만 수확한 뒤 아프리칸 베드에서 single layer 로 천천히 건조시켰습니다.
달콤한 복숭아 플레이버를 베이스로 프로세싱과 Q.C 를 진행한 내츄럴 원두입니다.

beans.name: 과테말라 리고 데 아티틀란(Traditional Atitlán)
beans.description:
정보가 부족
단일품목이아니고 교잡종인듯
전통적 아티틀란

beans.name: 에티오피아 리무 베르소나 내추럴
beans.description:

beans.description:

beans.name: 르완다 부산제 부르봉 워시드
beans.description:
오랜만에 소개드리는 르완다 입니다. 르완다를 많이 소개 못드리는 이유는 르완다에서 종종 나타나는 감자 느낌의 뉘앙스가 개인적으로 너무 안맞아서 소개드리기 힘들었는데요, 이번에 소개 드리는 원두는 이러한 부정적인 느낌을 제외하고 장점만 쏙 가져온 느낌입니다.
이번에 소개해드리는 부산제는 흡사 뉘앙스는 케냐와 비슷하지만 에티오피아 워시드에서 종종 보이는 플로럴함과 복숭복숭 느낌을 함께 섞었다고 보시면 될 듯 합니다.
맛에서는 초반에 복숭아틱한 느낌으로 시작해 자몽의 쌉싸름한 맛을 지나 꿀 같은 시러피함이 돋보입니다.
식었을 때도 불에 구운 자몽 같은 진득한 단맛을 보여주기에 아프리카 계열 원두를 좋아하시는 분들이라면 만족하실 듯 합니다.
르완다라는 생소한 나라의 좋은 생두를 고객님들께 소개시켜드릴 수 있어서 정말 기쁩니다.

beans.name: 파푸아뉴기니 보카 워시드
beans.description:
이 원두를 테스팅 할 때 들었던 생각이 잘 가공된 과테말라 원두 같다 였습니다. 파푸아뉴기니는 3년전 라마리 이후 처음인데, 그만큼 좋은 생두를 찾기 힘듭니다. 이번에 소개드리는 보카는 꽤 좋은 퀄리티를 보여주어 판매하게 되었습니다.
맛에서 고소함과 단맛을 장점으로 보여주는 원두는 오랜만 인 듯 합니다. 향미는  플로럴 복숭아와 맛에서 중간에 사탕수수 같은 단맛과 후미 아몬드 같은 고소함이 정말 매력적으로 다가와 저 또한 놀래기도 했습니다.
먹고 난 후의 후미의 여운이 꽤 긴 편이며 입안에서 고소한 향미가 뿜뿜한다고 해도 과언이 아닐듯 합니다. 신맛 단맛과 고소함이 너무 밸런스 좋게 느껴져 호불호가 없을 듯 합니다.
좋은 과테말라를 찾으시는 고객님들께서 한번 접해보시면 이 커피의 매력에 빠져들 것 같습니다.

beans.name: 온두라스 엘 도밍고 부르봉 워시드
beans.description:
항상 느낀점에 적지만 정말 올해 생산된 온두라스는 대부분 거를 게 없는 좋은 퀄리티를 가지고 있다고 생각합니다.
이 온두라스 또한 그러하며 단순하면서 자기주장 강한 친구 같은 는낌입니다.
향미는 오렌지 단 향이며 추출향은 청사과 입니다.
맛은 복숭아가 주력이며 클린컵이 좋아 복숭아 티같은 깔끔한 느낌을 줍니다.  깔끔함 뒤에 오는 단맛은 사탕수수를 오래 씹으면 나오는 단맛같이 진득합니다.
식으면 식을수록 은은한 시트러스함이 더해지는데, 이는 흡사 오렌지 쥬스같은 쥬시함을 표현합니다.
심심하지 않고 모난데 없이 밸런스 좋은 원두입니다. 많은 분들께 사랑받기를 바랍니다.

beans.name: 콜롬비아 카우카 엘 베르엘 워시드
beans.description:
져희 스마트 스토어에서 원두를 구매하시는 분들께서 꼭 한번씩은 구매 해보셨으면 하는 정말 좋은 원두를 소개합니다. 이 커피는 그 중 탑 급이라 생각하는데, 커핑노트를 훑어보시면 이 가격대에 나오기 힘든 노트들로만 구성되어있습니다.
홀빈향은 가득한 꽃향기로 시작해 분쇄향은 샴페인같은 경쾌한 느낌이며 맛에서는 백포도와 화이트 와인이 주 입니다.
식어갈수록 마카다미아 같은 견과류 톤의 단맛이 올라와 산미와 조화가 참 좋으며 식어갈수록 엿기름 같은 단맛이 치고 들어 옵니다. 맛과 향이 전후반 모두 가득 찬 느낌이고 자기만의 개성이 가득한 원두라 생각합니다.
산미와 단맛 밸런스가 너무 좋아 핫 아이스 모두 잘 어울리며 호불호가 없으면서 특별함을 주는 듯 합니다. 요즘같은 귀한 생두 찾기 힘든 시기에 많은 분들께서 즐겨 주셨으면 합니다.

beans.name: Ethiopia Shantawene
beans.description:
**TASTING NOTES:** Strawberry, Mango, Papaya & Cocoa
**ORIGIN:** Shantawene Sidama
**PROCESS:** Anaerobic Natural
**VARIETAL:** Heirloom
**ELEVATION:** 1900- 2100m
**Suggested Listening:** "Maybe" Kettenkarussell
Thanks to the robust presence of the Sidama Coffee Farmers Cooperative Union and standardized washed processing across its more than 50 cooperatives, the vast majority of its coffee is fully washed. As a result, Sidama coffee is often sought after for its delicate fruits, herbaceousness and buoyant citric acid and versatility,
But that is certainly not all that Sidama has to offer. More and more we are seeing younger producer groups set themselves up outside the union infrastructure and offer new, more cutting edge coffees into the market.
This particular coffee comes from a single 35-hectare estate owned by Faysel Abdosh. Faysel purchased the property in 2020 and immediately set to work dialing in processing systems he felt best suited the farm’s high elevation and selective cultivars.
The resulting lot, one of the first ever produced, busts Sidama coffee norms for three big reasons.
First, it comes from a private estate managed entirely by Faysel from tree to export.
Two the coffee has gone through an anaerobic process, which is VERY RARE for Ethiopia as a whole and not just the Sidama region.
Third and finally, the coffee is a Grade 1, meaning the cup quality and physical preparation is the best that Ethiopia certifies, something especially difficult to achieve.
Faysel’s farm is in the Bensa district, in far eastern Sidama Zone, on the outskirts of the Harenna Forest National Park. Faysel oversees a business team of 15 year round, and a crew of 150 during harvest and processing. Faysel exports coffee via Testi Coffee, a private exporter who represents a number of processors in Eastern Sidama, whose coffees are always among the most exquisite we taste all year.
This particular lot was processed using a unique cherry fermentation step prior to drying. Faysel uses a particular blend of cherry ripeness, combining 85 % fully-ripe, 10% over-ripe, and 5% under-ripe cherry together for processing, so as to balance sugars and acids in the final lot. Once fresh-picked cherry is blended to spec and floated to remove all low density coffee, the cherry is allowed to rest submerged underwater for 8 hours, which Faysel believes helps to initiate sugar breakdown inside the cherry and sweeten the final coffee. After the soaking is complete, cherry is laid out to dry on shaded raised beds at a depth of 5cm, where they are constantly rotated for 28 days.
We taste a plethora of tropical fruits encased in a rich decadent cacao.
If your a fan of the Ethiopian naturals, this is kind of of like an Ethiopian Natural on steroids - everything that you either love or hate of that kind of coffee is taken up to 11 here. Needless to say we here at modcup LOVE this coffee!

beans.name: Colombia Cafe La Granja Cerro Azul Natural Gesha

components.name: Cafe La Granja Cerro Azul Natural Gesha
components.description: Colombia Cafe La Granja Cerro Azul Natural Gesha

beans.description:
**TASTING NOTES:** Pineapple, Lemongrass, Blueberry
**ORIGIN:** Valle del Cauca, Colombia
**PROCESS:** Natural
**VARIETAL:** Gesha
**ELEVATION:** 1700- 2000m
**Suggested Listening: "Body Nature" Paula Tape**
Produced by world famous Colombian producers Cafe la Granja in the Valle Del Cauca at Finca Cerro Azul and entirely from trees of the Gesha variety of Arabica. This is a competition offering that has us all beyond excited here at modcup.
Gesha is the gold standard of coffee varietals. Originally  a coffee plant indiginous to East Africa it has found optimum growing conditions in the higher altitude and micro climates of Central and South America.
There's simply nothing else like Gesha in the coffee world. Incredibly delicate, floral and sweet, if you have never tried it, it's truly revelatory.
In 2019 modcup finished 12th in the world in coffee reviews list for best roasted coffee with a Gesha from Cafe La Granja and their Cerro Azul farm. That Gesha was a washed process.
This 2022 geisha offering has gone through a natural process and is UBER exotic.
As a result of the processing, fruit flavors dominate the usual florality that one associates with the Gesha Varietal.  We taste Pineapple, Berries and Lemongrass. A pronounced sweetness, a nice balance of malic and citric acidity, a silky body, and long long finish on the palate.
Fancy competing in your own home, then this is the competition coffee for you.

beans.name: COSTA RICA VOLCAN AZUL ANAEROBIC SL-28
beans.description:
Alejo C. Jiménez is a fifth generation coffee producer that comes from a long lineage of incredible agriculture. Using a state-of-the-art, 5 day anaerobic ferment processing method, this coffee carries loud notes of sophisticated berries, honey, and a mango drinking yogurt. This coffee is special and not to be missed.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ● | Modern
*Anaerobic Natural. Raised Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Raspberry Candy, Raw Honey, Mango Lassi, Floral
**STORY**
F.C.J. VOLCAN AZUL
“In the middle of the 19th century, when coffee production was in its early beginnings in America, without knowing it, two pioneers and entrepreneurs, Alejo C. Jiménez in Costa Rica and Wilhelm Kahle in the south of Mexico shared the same dream: “To produce the best coffee in the world” to satisfy the new demanding European gourmet market.
More than a century has passed and today the fourth and fifth generations of descendants of these visionary farmers still produce coffee within the same ideals of excellency and top quality that inspired their ancestors. They produce one of the best pure coffees of the world with its Brand “F.C.J. Volcan Azul” on the slopes of the Poás Volcano in Costa Rica.
Today, the production process starts with the planting of the coffee trees on highly fertile volcanic soil above 1400 Mts above sea level (SHB). It continues with a meticulous process at the coffee mill and finally ends with a strict preparation of the export qualities, which are roasted to reach the final consumer.
Nowadays, the descendants of Don Alejo C. and Don Wilhelm want to enhance further the principles of quality inherited by our founders by adding the value of conservancy of natural resources, through the acquisition of extensions of natural rainforest for its protection and conservation. These facts, not just words, are small actions taken by one family to reduce air contamination and global warming. This is the contribution we want to make to mankind, this is the new awareness we want to inherit to our future generations to inherit. For our family, the conservation of natural resources is of prime value, which is why we are committed to protecting vast extensions of tropical rainforest in Costa Rica since the ’80s. We have come to acquire over 1,500 hectares of rainforest for ecological preservation in areas where we grow our coffee as well as in the Osa Peninsula, a region in the South of Costa Rica is known for holding 2.5% of the Earth’s Biodiversity, proud to know that our family is contributing to reducing the effects of global warming. This is the attitude towards the environment that we want to pass on to our children and future generations of our family.” - Volcan Azul
ANAEROBIC PROCESSING
There are few leaps in the technique of processing coffee larger than the most recent trend of Anaerobic processing. This is oftentimes a technique of natural processing coffee, where whole cherries are fermented in a limited oxygen environment. The word Anaerobic is a catch-all term used more often than not to refer to a limited oxygen environment. Coffee cherries are subject to a pre-fermentation before they are moved to a drying bed or patio. The extended fermentation often adds a winey or fruity note to the coffee. Throughout the world, we’ve seen this practiced in many ways, from adding cherries into a clean grainpro and tying the top for the night, all the way to stainless tanks with an airlock on the top to slowly let out carbon dioxide caused by the microbes at work. There are many who are working to understand and better control this process to gain clarity on what is really going on with limited oxygen fermentation. Here is what Alejo from Volcan Azul says about their Anaerobic process:
“... Like all varieties we choose the right moment to start harvesting it, measuring Brix content. Then we bring the cherries to the wet mill where we separate floaters and wash the cherries so they are clean before going into the anaerobic tanks. In this case, we left the coffee five days inside the fermentation tanks, measuring temperature, Brix content, acidity (ph), and time. After this, it was taken to the raised beds for 11 days and then 8 hours in a mechanical dryer (Guardiola).”

beans.name: ETHIOPIA BASHA BEKELE ZERO O2 NATURAL

components.name: BASHA BEKELE ZERO O2 NATURAL
components.description: ETHIOPIA BASHA BEKELE ZERO O2 NATURAL

beans.description:
Basha Bekele is a prodigious coffee producer hailing from the famous Bombe Village in Bensa, Ethiopia. This experimental zero oxygen natural has been fermented anaerobically, which has significantly intensified the raspberry and citric notes of this offering. As this coffee cools, you'll find a lasting sweetness of a fresh fig with the delicate tannins of black tea.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ● | Modern
*Anaerobic Natural, Raised Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Tart Raspberry, Lemon, Black Tea, Fresh Fig
**STORY**
BASHA BEKELE by Catalyst Trade
In 2014, we began working with Asnake Bekele. Now, you might recognize Asnake because of his signature modest, gold-toothed grin--but you might not know that he is single-handedly responsible for us all having the privilege of drinking (importing, roasting, serving) quality natural-processed Sidama coffee! Many years ago, when Asnake worked as the manager of the prominent Sidama Coffee Farmer's Cooperative Union, he presented the idea to the producers to add raised-bed natural processing to their washed processed coffee. At the time, he was laughed at! But luckily for us all, he persevered, and of course now we all know the glory of natural-processed Sidama coffee.
While Asnake's career in Ethiopian coffee has spanned many high points, we've had the privilege of partnering with him on a number of projects which included groundbreaking processing work in Kochere and Gelana Abaya (together we introduced honey processing) as well as on a researched article which directly resulted in over $8 million USD being invested by the Ethiopian government into the Gedeo Zone to repair dozens of washing stations damaged in the 2017 riots. He also helped us organize several producer groups in various parts of Ethiopia as well as served as an incredibly wise Ethiopian coffee expert and resource to us. When he began his latest endeavor--partnering with outstanding Sidama single producers such as Basha Bekele-we were on board from the first moment, and in fact we have the privilege of being his very first collaborative partner and of building this program with him. Basha Bekele is part of the producing group Asnake and his partner Aklilu Admassu has vertically integrated with. He's focused on quality and on advancing the future of traceable, single-producer Ethiopian coffee. In a recent event co-hosted by Asnake's company and Catalyst Trade, Basha spent two days with us learning details of advanced processing, financial management, and many other aspects of successful coffee production before taking to a boat to enjoy the bright sun and the hippo sightings on the Great Rift Valley Lake of Hawassa, where we held the retreat.
Asnake shares that on Basha's farm, "Red Cherries are harvested by hand-picking. This is very labor intensive, and around 86 seasonal and daily laborers are employed for selective harvesting, transporting, sorting and drying. Pickers many a time have to return to the same tree multiple times as coffee cherry doesn't ripen all at the same time. Carefully harvested cherries are then loaded into bags or baskets and taken to the drying location. The cherries are spread out in thin layers to dry in the sun. Only special raised drying beds or tables, which are made out of wood posts, are used and covered in bamboo mat. In order to ensure even drying and to avoid mold, fermentation or rotting, the cherries are turned 6 times per day. It could take 15 - 21 days for specific lots to reach optimum moisture content depending on the sun access and temperature. This is determined by observing brightness of the skin and cracking sample dried cherries by teeth. When the drying is complete, the dried cherries are loaded into poly baqs and stored in temporary warehouse. It will then ultimately transported to the dry mill and warehouse located at Day town." At that point the dried cherry is removed and a phase of pre-cleaning is done by hand before the coffee is loaded into bags for transportation to Addis Ababa for further export preparation at the final dry mill.
**ZERO O2 NATURAL**
In an effort to better understand and control the processing experiments that Catalyst Trade has been doing at the Abore site, they invested in some new equipment at the outset of the harvest. This equipment, including sealed tanks for processing whole cherries, allowed for better control of free oxygen in the fermentation environment. This natural process bombe lot was processed within those barrels with an airlock mechanism, allowing for free oxygen to be flushed out as the microbes created CO2 during their breakdown of sugars. After a few conversations with Catalyst, we were driven to label this as a zero oxygen natural process, which allows for the distinction of the other limited oxygen naturals that took place this season as well. (Keep an eye out for those to be released soon.) After the whole cherries are fermented in a sealed barrel for a 24 hour cycle, the cherries are then removed and dried on shaded raised beds much like the other naturals at the site. This limited oxygen cycle creates a wild jam-like note in the cup, with subtle florals on the aroma and the palate reminiscent of the terroir of Bensa coffees.

beans.description:
Land of Diversity Auction 은 콜롬비아 전역에서 출품된 커피로 1년에 2회 개최됩니다.
Neyibia Cuellar Cuellar 는 무려 이 대회의 1위 커피 입니다. Neyibia 는 여성 농부로, 그녀는 이미 경제학자로서 성공했지만 아버지가 돌아가신 후 커피 농사를 이어가고 싶어 농장으로 돌아왔습니다.
그녀는 커피 농장에서 태어나 자연스럽게 커피 생선의 모든 과정을 접하며 살았고, 커피에 대한 열정이 강했기에 짧은 기간에도 좋은 커피를 키워낼 수 있었습니다.
특별한 종자나 프로세싱이 아님에도 1위를 차지했다는 건 그녀의 노력과 열정이 그만큼 담겨있는 커피라는 생각이 들게 합니다.
## 주인장 느낀점
이 원두를 테스팅 하면서 커핑 노트를 작성하는데 2분도 안 걸린 듯 합니다. 그만큼 커핑노트가 뚜렷하며 직관적이고 머리에 딱 들어오는 느낌입니다.
열대과일의 향미와 오렌지 초콜릿 순서대로 들어오며 리치한 홀빈향과 단호박같은 커피의 질감은 이 커피가 산미가 좋으면서 묵직하고 달달해서 밸런스있고 호불호 없이 고급짐을 느끼게 해준다고 생각합니다.
위에도 설명했듯이 1위라는 순위에 일반 보통의 품종에 보통의 가공방식이 랭크 되었다는 뜻은 그만큼 본 토양에서 나온 생두 자체가 퀄리 좋으며 프로듀서의 생두 수확과 관리가 일품이었다 라고 해도 과언이 아닐 듯 합니다.
많은 분들이 한번쯤 선택하셔서 즐겨 주셨스면 합니다.

beans.name: 콜롬비아 하이로 아르실라 민트
beans.description:
허니 + 민트 발효된 커피입니다.
펄프를 벗긴 커피 체리에 타르타르산과 민트 잎을 넣어 72시간동안 드라이 무산소 발효한 뒤 수분 함햘이 10.5%가 될 때까지 건조시킨다.
홀빈향부터 화하게 올라오는 민트향이 커피를 분쇄할때, 커피에 물을 부었을 때, 커피를 마실때까지 쭉 이어지는 민트의 특징이 일괄성이 좋은 커피입니다.
명확한 페퍼민트, 박하의 화하고 시원한 느낌이 특징적입니다. 페퍼민트 차를 마시는 듯한 느낌이며, 레몬그라스의 산뜻한 느낌과 백도복숭아 같은 과일의 산미가 느껴집니다.
이러한 독특한 특징들이 잘 느껴지면서 단맛이 잘 받쳐주어 밸런스가 정말 좋다고 느껴졌습니다. 질감은 입속에서 굴렁굴렁한 느낌이 실키하다고 느껴졌습니다.
민트의 느낌을 좋아하시거나, 커피에서 독특하고 신선한 경험을 원하시는 분들께 적극 추천드립니다.

beans.name: COSTA RICA LAS LAJAS YELLOW HONEY
beans.description:
Over the course of three harvest we've dialed in our honey selection from the famed Las Lajas farm. This season we've honed in on the yellow diamond process, which exudes tropical fruit notes while maintaining a structured body akin to thick honey.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ○ | Modern
*Yellow Diamond Process, Patio Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Strawberry Jam, Dried Mango, Hibiscus, Honey
**STORY**
The Las Lajas micromill is located in the Sabanilla de Alajuela region of Costa Rica. Francisca and Oscar Chacon greet you in the driveway with huge smiles and a willingness to show you their stunning farms. Their pride is evident as they show you each immaculate step the coffee goes through. It all starts with their farming practices; Las Lajas is a certified organic farm. These third-generation producers focus on preserving the environment while maintaining the highest quality coffee. Their micromill is immaculately clean, keeping the processing standards they are well known for; the Chacons were some of the first to do high-quality naturals and honeys in Central America. Going into the 2020 harvest, they completed a renovation of their mill, complete with a corkscrew conveyor belt that transports depulped coffee to a custom trailer, which transports it to their drying facility. Strict size and density separation happen at the mills, even the naturals are passed through the mill to separate the less ripe and small size cherries after selective harvesting.
The last step of quality and care takes place on the drying patios and beds. This is where we believe Las Lajas is truly advancing coffee. Oscar told us that he strives to focus on drying the green coffee the same as a roaster focuses on roasting by paying close attention to curves and data. They have become so precise in their processing that they have been able to produce several offerings under various monikers; this particular coffee is their Yellow-Honey Diamond. This is a honey-process coffee by which coffee is depulped, then added to the drying patios in a large pile to increase activity in fermentation. As the coffee dries, it is piled up, and then flattened out to slow the process.
**HONEY PROCESSED COFFEE**
Honey processed coffees are tricky. Oftentimes the result of this process ends up being the same as if they were washed, but other times they end up much worse. The process begins much like washed coffee, where the cherries are depulped, but then the process is halted. Instead of sending the seeds to the fermentation tanks where yeast and microbes break down the sticky mucilage, the honey processed coffee is sent straight to the drying bed, where they’re pop dried with the mucilage left on. This leaves some of the fruit left partially on, ideally imparting some sucrose and fructose as it continues to ferment. (slowly, in this case.) Honey process gets its name from the sticky fruit left on the outside of the seed, but it is easier to think about when it’s referred to as a ‘pulped natural,’ or even ‘partially washed.’ Differing levels of fermentation and even the type of depulper used to strip the cherry from the seed can influence the color of the dried coffee. As the process's popularity has spread, new names for the levels of processing have spread with it. You will see the levels generally follow the trend from white honey, all the way to black honey.
White honey usually follows the trend that most of the mucilage is removed, followed by a light and short fermentation with rigorous movement during drying. This exposes most of the parchment on the seed, which is a yellow to white color.
Yellow honey has a bit more mucilage left on the seed, as well as a bit more activity in fermentation. The mucilage left on will turn a golden yellow color as the oxidation of fermentation takes place. These are usually shade-dried and turned just a few times per day of drying.
Red honey has much of the seeds mucilage left on, with just the thin cherries skin removed during depulp. Heavily laden drying beds or patios become difficult to turn with the large mass of sticky fruit and seeds, which creates the perfect conditions for slow oxidation and lots of microbial activity. All this activity, combined with the fruit left on the outside of the seed, turns the dried parchment a rusty red color.
Black honeys are considered the most ‘fermented’ of the honey process. This is when the terroir of the coffee takes a bit of a backseat, as the sugars from the fruit ferment and impart a winey flavor and heavier texture to the cup. Black honey coffees are seldom moved on the drying patios or beds, where they are fermented for extended periods of time. During this slow fermentation, the mucilage goes through all the phases listed above, before it transitions from a rusty red to a red-black color.

beans.name: COLOMBIA JUAN JIMENEZ

components.name: JUAN JIMENEZ PINK BOURBON Washed & Raised Bed Dried
components.description: COLOMBIA JUAN JIMENEZ PINK BOURBON Washed & Raised Bed Dried

beans.description:
**STORY**
This unique coffee comes from southern Huila, from Juan Jimenez. Sr. Jimenez grows Caturra, Colombia, and Pink Bourbon on his 16 hectare farm. Each variety is meticulously picked and processed with quality in mind. Both Juan and his wife Leidy manage the farm, taking care to reinvest earnings into the farm each harvest in order to sustain high quality harvests for years to come. Kyle at Osito has been working with El Porvenir for two years, buying a large amount of all the varieties produced on their farm. This season was marks our second year purchasing not only Juan’s pink bourbon, but his caturra as well.
**PINK BOURBON**
This is a very recently discovered variety found in Acevedo, Huila. Pink Bourbon, or Borbón Rosado, is touted as a hybridization of Red and Yellow Bourbon. On the cupping table, this variety can sometimes be mistaken for an Ethiopian coffee, with its floral notes and it’s delicate tea-like nature. Since it is a new discovery, little research has been done to confirm it’s background or origin story, but that has not stopped it from exploding in popularity both with roasters and producers. While it’s less rare that it once was, finding an extremely high quality Pink Bourbon offering is just as difficult, due to its specific growing and picking requirements. Much like Yellow Bourbon, Pink Bourbon is difficult to identify at peak ripeness. Red Bourbon is much easier, as cherries transition from bright red to deep purple within a matter of a week or more. Pink Bourbon can be much more nuanced than it’s red counterpart, making it difficult to determine the underripe from the mature. With careful picking and processing however, the nuanced nature of Pink Bourbon can be translated into a tremendously delicate and unique cup of coffee.

beans.name: ANIMAL COLLECTIVE
beans.description:
We're excited to release this collaboration with one of our all time favorite groups of artists, Animal Collective. Sampled and hand chosen by the band, this collaborative offering is an ode to creativity and inspiration and a representation of our shared love for experimentation and a curiosity for all types of sensory exploration... This harvest combines multiple citrus and stone fruits with delicate florals.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ○ | Modern
*Washed & Raised-Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Pomelo, Elderflower, Black Tea, Lemon
**STORY**
This majestic heirloom variety is grown by our dear friend, Aman Adinew at Metad coffee. This is our seventh year to offer this award winning coffee from the select northern micro-lot known as Buku. Within our near eight-year relationship, both Tariku and Aman have proven to care deeply about their coffees, and about personal relationships. Tariku Adinew has the gift of hospitality, lending to harmony in their coffees, and the efforts of their family work even surpass the experience that buyers have with them and their coffees. That experience is evident in the cup, with its delicate florals and soft fruit characters, but it moves far deeper than the taste experience of the coffees.
Over the last two seasons, METAD has opened up a state-of-the-art dry milling facility where all of our coffees are milled and prepped for export. This is advantageous for everyone, as the lines can be long at other milling facilities. (and fraught with issues) This has allowed our sourcing program to stay nimble in the last two challenging seasons. The milling facility is located strategically outside of the city, with a direct road circumventing the busy capital of Addis Ababa. From the facility, the stuffed 20-foot containers preferred for coffee shipping can take a quick two-day journey to the port of Djibouti, and thus onward, eventually arriving at our roastery in Arkansas. You can read more about this journey in the transparency section below.
**Washed Process**
The washed process begins with the coffee cherries being delivered to the washing station. The cherries are inspected, and an initial quick round of hand-sorting separates the defective cherries before placing them into the hopper. They are then funneled to the Penagos pulper, which removes the fruit from the seeds. This is a crucial step to allowing the seed to ferment and become an exportable green seed ready for roasting. After the de-pulping phase, the seeds are then fermented underwater for approximately 12-24 hours. This phase of fermentation is crucial, as the east and microbes are breaking down the stir y mucilage layer found on the outside of the seed following depulp. Once the 24-hour fermentation is complete, the parchment is emptied into the washing channels, where it is agitated with rates. During this step, the water is refreshed to ensure proper aviation and washing is taking place. Once the washing is complete, the coffee is taken to the raised drying tables for sun drying.

beans.name: ETHIOPIA NEGUSSE NARE LOW O2
beans.description:
Hailing from the Bensa region, Negusse Nare's six-hectare farm produces all the florals and sweetness we love from Bensa coffees. This experimental pushes the level of fermentation, while retaining the beautiful florals we expect from Bensa.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ● | Modern
*Anaerobic Natural & Raised Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Raspberry Pureé, Honeysuckle, Blackberry Compote, Black
Tea
**STORY**

(Keep an eye out for those to be released soon.) beans.name: MEXICO JOSÉ ARGUELLO GESHA
beans.description:
**MEXICO JOSÉ ARGUELLO (GESHA)**
**mandarin, vanilla, butterscotch, jasmine**
****
****
****
Finca Santa Cruz Honey Gesha
Location: Chiapas
Elevation: 1700m
Varieties: Gesha
Process: Honey
****
****
**도안 코멘트**
****
이 멕시코 게이샤는 올해 하트에서 출시하는 마지막 게이샤 입니다.
하트는 이 게이샤를 다른 파트너에게 판매하지 않고 자신의 매장과 유일하게 도안에게만
판매 권한을 주었습니다. 저희는 이 게이샤를 모두 구매하겠다고 했고
여러분과 같이 나누기 위해 노력했습니다.
****
**Heart** - It is the Mexico Jose Arguello - Honey Processed Gesha with notes of mango, praline, and apple blossom.
*"The producer won this year's Mexico COE with a different Natural processed lot."*
*'이 커피는 멕시코 호세 아르구엘로 입니다. 망고, 프랄린, 사과꽃 향의 컵노트 이며 허니 가공 게이샤입니다. 이 프로듀서는 다른 Natural 가공랏으로 올해 멕시코 COE에서 우승했습니다.'*
**
**
**
******
독점으로 받은 이 게이샤를
같이 즐겨주세요. 감사합니다 :-)
****

beans.name: GUATEMALA ROSMA PACAMARA,MARAGOGYPE
beans.description:
**GUATEMALA ROSMA**
**honeydew, clementine, caramel**
****
Location: Huehuetenango
Varieties: Pacamara, Maragogipe
Process: Fully washed
Elevation: 1600-1900m

beans.name: 에티오피아 구지 샤키소 테로 워시드
beans.description:
바닐라톤이 선명한 커피 입니다. 농익은 과일과 바닐라가 주 노트 이며 크리미한 질감이 함꼐 따라옵니다.
플로럴한 홀빈 향 다음 블루베리와 바닐라톤의 향미가 따라오며 바닐라톤의 실키한 텍스쳐가 정말 매력적으로 다가옵니다. 에티오피아 워시드 하면 떠오르는 꽃차 느낌보다 묵직하고 단맛이 잘 받쳐주어 마치 겨울을 위한 에티오피아 워시드가 아닌가 싶습니다.
커피가 식어가도 유지력이 좋아 탄탄하다 라는 느낌을 받았으며 잘 익은 라임 같은 산미가 올라오는데 시큼하거나 새콤하지 않고 은은하게 다가와 입가심을 하는 듯 합니다.
저번에 판매했던 고구구의 업그레이드 버전이라 보셔도 될 듯 합니다. 추운 겨울에 어울리는 에티오피아 커피로 따듯하게 보내셨으면 합니다.

beans.name: 에티오피아 봄베 파이젤 압도쉬 내추럴
beans.description:
많은 로스터리에서 판매중인 원두 입니다. 많은 사람들이 선택했다면 이유가 있다고 생각합니다. 그 이유에 저도 공감하는 부분이라 판매하게 되었는데요, 발효취 없이 깔끔하며 다양한 노트들이 뿜어져 나오는 매력만점 내추럴 커피입니다.
플로럴함과 과일스러움, 초콜리티 하면서 모카와 자스민 톤 등이 이 아이가 자기오 있는 특징은 꽤나 많은 듯 하빈다. 좋은 커피는 다양한 노트들이 직관적으로 느낄 수 있게 나오고, 잡미 없이 깔끄하게 떨어지며 임팩트를 가지고 있는 아이라 생각하는데, 딱 이 커피가 이러한 느낌입니다.
무엇보다 식어갈 수록 경괘하게 느끼실 수 있고 청량하게 느끼실 수 있는 느낌은 저에게도 특별하게 다가왔는데요, 다양하고 많은 과일, 꽃 등을 먹은 후 입안을 개운하게 행구는 느낌이라 이색적이며 긍정적으로 다가왔습니다.
물온도, 추출온도, 내리는 형태에 따라 다양한 캐릭터를 뽐내는 듯 합니다. 저와 같은 기분좋은 경험 함께 나눠 주시길 바랍니다.

beans.name: 엘살바도르 산타로사 파카마라 내추럴
beans.description:
작년에 많은 사랑을 받은 산타로사 파카마라 내추럴 입니다. 올해는 조금 달라진 모습으로 입고되었는데요, 애깅ㄴ적으로 작년보다 더 마음에 들어 판매하게 되었습니다.
향미부분에서는 열대과일과 와이니한 느낌이 섞인 듯한 느낌입니다. 과한 발효가 아니어서 은은한 과일향과 약간의 와이니한 느낌이 정말 매력적입니다. 향미는 파인애플, 오렌지, 적포도 순으로 나오는데 여전히 와이니한 느낌이 조미료처럼 첨가되어 맛있게 다가옵니다.
맛에서는 체리같은 산미에 미끄러운 질감, 다음 달달하며 꺠끗한 단맛이 들어오는데, 이 부분에서 잡미가 없어 엄청 투명하게 느껴집니다.
식었을 때는 산미와 단맛이 함께 치고 들어와 좋은 밸런스를 보여주며 엘살에 대한 편견을 깨줍니다. 가격이 있는 생두인 만큼 생두 퀄리티도 좋았으며, 다양한 향미와 좋은 밸런스 그리고 깔끔한 단맛을 좋아하시는 분들이라면 적극 추천 드립니다.

beans.name: 브라질 파젠다 프리미사오
beans.description:
**★ 고소한 견과류 플레이버의 기본을 보여주는 커피입니다.**
**★** **400g 구매 시 200g 2봉이 발송됩니다.**
**★ 홀빈 아그트론 : 65-69, (High Roast)**
**★ 디개싱은 약 5~7일 권장합니다.**
**★ 모든 추출 레시피에 잘 나오도록 로스팅 했습니다.**
**★ 받으시는 커피는 전일 혹은 주문 당일 로스팅 제품을 발송합니다.**
**★ 원두(홀빈) 상태로 패킹 되기 때문에 분쇄 요청 시 상단면 개봉 흔적이 있습니다.**

beans.description: .jpg?type=w860)

beans.name: 온두라스 엘 인헤르또 카투아이 워시드
beans.description:
추운 겨울 함께하면 좋은 커피를 소개합니다. 겨울하면 떠오르는 노트를 두르 가지고 있습니다. 봉투를 열었을 때 은은하게 퍼지는 딸기 크림, 달달한 단감과 초콜릿을 섞은 듯한 분쇄향, 그리고 향긋한 시나몬 등 한 겨울에 잘 어울리는 노트가 나옵니다.
맛에서는 첫 모금 시나몬이 느껴집니다. 그 다음 붉은 사과 같은 산미가 살짝 올라오고 설탕물 같은 느낌의 단맛이 올라옵니다. 단맛은 끈적거리는 시럽 느낌이 아닌 꿀떡꿀떡 넘어가는 설탕물 같습니다.
시간이 지날수록 신기한 경험을 했는데요, 커피가 점점 식어갈 수록 설탕물 같은 단맛이 크리미하게 느껴지며 단감 또는 홍시같은 느낌을 주었습니다.
단맛은 여전한데 질감이 변하는 느낌은 생소했어서 재미난 경험이었습니다. 약간의 산미와 좋은 다남ㅅ으로 밸런스를 유지하며 개성있는 노트를 원하시는 분들 께 반갑게 다가갈 듯 합니다.

beans.name: 니카라과 골더 아워
beans.description:
\# Golder Hour
산미와 복합성에 초점을 맞춰 CM프로파일을 구성해 짧은 시간 발효를 진행했습니다. 이후 체리를 꺼내 아프리칸 베드에 얇게 펴서 건조했습니다.
이후 아프리칸 베드에 체리를 쌓아 건조를 진행했습니다.
저번 니카라과 리틀레드라이딩후드를 좋아하셨던 고객님이시라면 이번 골더아워도 만족하실 듯 합니다. 향미 부분은 체리와 와인 범벅입니다.
추출할 때 레몬 와이니한 향미 뒤에 레몬 그라스가 살짝 나오는데 잘 어우러져 복합적으로 느껴집니다.  이 커피는 맛에서 세가지로 요약할 수 있을 듯 합니다.
체리 자두 초콜릿 이빈다. 산미는 은은한 검붉은 체리 산미와 중간에 자두 같은 향미가 뿅 나타납니다. 후미는 밀크 초콜릿과 식었을 때도 달달하면서 초콜릿 후미가 입안을 맴돕니다.
입안에 달라붙는듯한 단맛 또한 이 커피의 매력입니다. 올해 들어온 니카라과는 모두 마음에 들었지만 특히나 더 마음에 드는 아이 같습니다. 추운 겨울 와이니한 느낌으로 따듯하게 즐기시면 더 할 나위 없을 듯 합니다.

beans.name: 예멘 마카드 후세인 자디 내추럴
beans.description:
로스터릭에서 처음으로 선보이는 예멘 커피 입니다.
예멘 커피하면 과거 3대 커피로 알려진 커피라 많은 커피 애호가들이 찾았는데요, 저는 2012년도 스페셜티를 처음 접하면서 후에 마신 예멘은 가격이 비싼 거품이 많은 커피로 인식이 되어 관심이 없었습니다. .시간이 흘러 인식이 바뀌면서 국내에 좋은 품질의 예멘이 들어와 많이 테스팅 했었는데요, 드디어 좋은 품질의 커피가 들어와 소개할 수 있어서 기쁩니다.
잘 가공된 내추럴 커피로 봉투를 개봉하면 얼그레이가 뿜뿜 합니다. 추출향미와 맛에서 노트대로 흘러가며 체리 산미에서 중간쯤 파일애플 향미와 산미가 올라오며 끝은 기분 좋은 카카오닙스로 끝이 납니다.
식어갈수록 예멘이 가지고 있는 모카 같은 느낌이 나오는데 이게 엄청 기분이 좋습니다. 향미나 맛 면에서 튀지는 않으나 강하다 라는 느낌이 듭니다.
더 이상 긴 설명이 필요 없을 듯 합니다. 주인장이 모든 고객님들께 적극적으로 추천 드립니다.

beans.name: ETHIOPIA DUMERSO LAYERED
beans.description:
A stunning micro-lot from our friends at Catalyst Trade, this experimental process is achieved by layering fresh de-pulped coffee over the course of a few days. The resulting cup is laden with fresh peach, balanced out with delicate tannins in the finish.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ○ | Modern
*Layered Fermentation Washed & Raised-Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Peach Candy, Green Apple, Confectioners Sugar, White Tea
****
**STORY**
DUMERSO WASHING STATION RELATIONSHIP by Catalyst Trade
Led by the dynamic sister team of CEO Hirut Birhanu and Manager Mahder Birhanu, Dumerso is a family endeavor that is anchored in Yirgacheffe and extends its reach to several other areas of Ethiopia. The flagship washing station is Dumerso, acquired 12 years ago and named for the village where it is located in the Yirgacheffe district. The family has lovingly worked in coffee for more than 20 years, in the process undergoing quite a lot of upheaval and real challenges.
One of the most stunning challenges they faced was one we documented for the global coffee world before we met Hirut and Mahder. (You can read that article at *Daily Coffee News*: “A Year of Turbulence and New Hope in Ethiopia’s Critically Important Gedeo Zone”) Piggybacking on racial tensions, agitators incited the local people to destroy dozens of washing stations owned by Ethiopians of other tribal backgrounds than Gedeo. Dumerso’s site was looted and destroyed. The family lost a lot of money in the process but remained hopeful that they could do good for the area and rebuild. In the same season, nightly low temperatures that hadn’t been seen in more than fifty years cause frost to strike the area and decimated the harvest. In spite of these challenges, with great fortitude, the family has continued to forge ahead. Ever since they have been rebuilding and in so doing have begun to focus on dialing in excellence in-process controls.
We began working with them in 2020, during an epic year of change and challenges. The coffee came in late in the season and wowed us every time on the cupping table with incredibly complex fruits like mandarin, earl grey, lemon sugar, and champagne grapes. This, combined with distinct floral and herbal notes such as white rose, bergamot, and eucalyptus, excited us and promised that we’d found the classic Yirgacheffe profile for the first time in a few years of searching for it. We immediately began to plan for the 2021 season, and our team has spent a lot of time on the ground with their team. Catalyst Trade Founder and Relationships & Processing Director Zelalem Girma Bayou says that he wishes more people were as careful and open to collaboration as the Industrial Manager at the Dumerso site.

beans.name: ETHIOPIA NEGUSSE NARE BOMBE NATURAL
beans.description:
Hailing from the famous Bensa region, Negusse Nare's six-hectare farm is producing all the florals and sweetness that we love from Bensa coffees. By allowing the fruit to dry on the seed, Negusse has achieved a heavy-bodied, fruity sweetness that accompanies the delicate florals of the heirloom variety. This naturally processed coffee is versatile across many different extraction types.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ○ ○ | Modern
*Natural & Raised-Bed Dried.*
**Cup Notes:** Honeysuckle, Golden Raisin, Dried Cherry, Malt
**STORY**
**Negusse Nare by Catalyst Trade**
In 2014, we began working with Asnake Bekele. Now, you might recognize Asnake because of his signature modest, gold-toothed grin—but you might not know that he is single-handedly responsible for us all having the privilege of drinking (importing, roasting, serving) quality natural-processed Sidama coffee! Many years ago, when Asnake worked as the manager of the prominent Sidama Coffee Farmer’s Cooperative Union, he presented the idea to
the producers to add raised-bed natural processing to their washed processed coffee. At the time, he was laughed at! But luckily for us all, he persevered, and of course, now we all know the glory of natural-processed Sidama coffee.
While Asnake’s career in Ethiopian coffee has spanned many high points, we’ve had the privilege of partnering with him on a number of projects which included groundbreaking processing work in Kochere and Gelana Abaya (together we introduced honey processing) as well as on a researched article which directly resulted in over $8 million USD being invested by the Ethiopian government into the Gedeo Zone to repair dozens of washing stations damaged in the 2017 riots. He also helped us organize several producer groups in various parts of Ethiopia as well as served as an incredibly wise Ethiopian coffee expert and resource to us. When he began his latest endeavor—partnering with outstanding Sidama single producers such as Negusse Nare—we were on board from the first moment, and in fact, we have the privilege of being his very first collaborative partner and of building this program with him.
Negusse Nare is part of the producing group Asnake and his partner Aklilu Admassu has vertically integrated with. He’s focused on quality and on advancing the future of traceable, single-producer Ethiopian coffee. In a recent event co-hosted by Asnake’s company and Catalyst Trade, Negusse spent two days with us learning details of advanced processing, financial management, and many other aspects of successful coffee production before taking to a boat to enjoy the bright sun and the hippo sightings on the Great Rift Valley Lake of Hawassa, where we held the retreat.
Asnake shares that on Negusse’s farm, “Red Cherries are harvested by hand-picking. This is very labor-intensive, and around 75 seasonal and daily laborers are employed for selective harvesting, transporting, sorting, and drying. Pickers many a time have to return to the same tree multiple times as coffee cherry doesn’t ripen all at the same time. Carefully harvested cherries are then are loaded into bags or baskets and taken to the drying location. The cherries are spread out in thin layers to dry in the sun. Only special raised drying beds or tables, which are made out of wood posts, are used and covered in a bamboo mat. In order to ensure even drying and to avoid mold, fermentation, or rotting, the cherries are turned 6 times per day. It could take 15 – 21 days for specific lots to reach optimum moisture content depending on the sun access and temperature. This is determined by observing the brightness of the skin and cracking sample dried cherries by teeth. When the drying is complete, the dried cherries are loaded into poly bags and stored in a temporary warehouse. It will then ultimately be transported to the dry mill and warehouse located at Daye town.” At that point, the dried cherry is removed and a phase of pre-cleaning is done by hand before the coffee is loaded into bags for transportation to Addis Ababa for further export preparation at the final dry mill.
**Naturally Processed Coffees**
Natural coffees are beautiful... Okay, natural coffees are beautiful when done properly, but can be equally terrible when things go wrong. Natural processing, or dry processing, refers to the act of drying and fermenting coffee inside the cherry. Long before the age of portafilter tattoos and dual-boiler home espresso machines, coffee was picked and dried this way out of convenience. It is, to this day, still the most convenient and economically friendly way to process coffee cherries. (It’s estimated that dry-processing can use up to 90% less water than the washing process.) So why isn’t all coffee processed this way? Well, as coffee made its way across the world, it was commoditized and standardized, just like all other products spread by colonialism, but that’s a whole other story... Adding to the boom of washed processing, the natural process method can be tricky to get right, due to the delicate nature of fermentation and drying. What does all this have to do with the final cup? Well, when you leave the skin and fruit of the coffee cherry on the seed throughout fermentation and drying, that fruit begins to break down, imparting esters that impart delicate florals and big fruit notes into the seed that survive the roasting process. If it’s rushed or handled incorrectly, this fruit rot can lend off-flavors to the coffee, making the final cup dirty or ‘fermenty.’ Basically, that single cherry begins to slowly decay, and controlling that delicate action through advanced technique and metrics allow us, lucky folks, to drink wonderfully floral and fruity coffees. We have long promoted natural processed coffees, and this Ethiopia Gedeb Beriti is just one of the reasons we do.

beans.name: COSTA RICA LAS LAJAS NATURAL
beans.description:
We are buzzing with excitement for our third harvest working with Las Lajas. The Chacons routinely produce wonderfully perfumed and sweet coffees, and this natural is no different. Expertly processed at their immaculately clean mill, this natural strikes a near-perfect balance of tactile and flavor complexity with notes of tart raspberry and a lingering sweetness of honey.
Traditional | ● ● ● ● ● ● ● ● | Modern
*Natural & Patio-Dried.*
**Cup Notes: Raspberry, Raw Honey, Papaya, Vanilla**
**STORY**
The Las Lajas micromill is located in the Sabanilla de Alajuela region of Costa Rica. Francisca and Oscar Chacon greet you in the driveway with huge smiles and a willingness to show you their stunning farms. Their pride is evident as they show you each immaculate step the coffee goes through. It all starts with their farming practices; Las Lajas is a certified organic farm. These third-generation producers focus on preserving the environment while maintaining the highest quality coffee. Their micromill is immaculately clean, keeping the processing standards they are well known for; the Chacons were some of the first to do high-quality naturals and honeys in Central America. Going into the 2020 harvest, they completed a renovation of their mill, complete with a corkscrew conveyor belt that transports depulped coffee to a custom trailer, which transports it to their drying facility. Strict size and density separation happen at the mills, even the naturals are passed through the mill to separate the less ripe and small size cherries after selective harvesting.
The last step of quality and care takes place on the drying patios and beds. This is where we believe Las Lajas is truly advancing coffee. Oscar told us that he strives to focus on drying the green coffee the same as a roaster focuses on roasting by paying close attention to curves and data. They have become so precise in their processing that they have been able to produce several offerings under various monikers; this particular coffee is their Perla Negra, a natural-process coffee by which whole cherries are turned hourly on raised beds.
**NATURALLY PROCESSED COFFEE**
Natural coffees are beautiful…Okay, natural coffees are beautiful when done properly, but can be equally terrible when things go wrong. Natural processing, or dry processing, refers to the act of drying and fermenting coffee inside the cherry. Long before the age of portafilter tattoos and dual-boiler home espresso machines, coffee was picked and dried this way out of convenience. It is, to this day, still the most convenient and economically friendly way to process coffee cherries. (It’s estimated that dry-processing can use up to 90% less water than the washing process.) So why isn’t all coffee processed this way? Well, as coffee made its way across the world, it was commoditized and standardized, just like all other products spread by colonialism, but that’s a whole other story... Adding to the boom of washed processing, the natural process method can be tricky to get right, due to the delicate nature of fermentation and drying. What does all this have to do with the final cup? Well, when you leave the skin and fruit of the coffee cherry on the seed throughout fermentation and drying, that fruit begins to break down, imparting esters that influence delicate florals and big fruit notes into the seed that survive the roasting process. If it’s rushed or handled incorrectly, this fruit rot can lend off-flavors to the coffee, making the final cup dirty or ‘fermenty.’ Basically that single cherry begins to slowly decay, and controlling that delicate action through advanced technique and metrics allow us, lucky folks, to drink wonderfully floral and fruity coffees. We have long promoted natural processed coffees, and this Costa Rica Las Lajas Natural is just one of the reasons we do.

beans.name: Kenya Kiambu Karinga

components.name: Kiambu Karinga SL28 & SL34 Washed
components.description: Kenya Kiambu Karinga SL28 & SL34 Washed

beans.description:
**Karinga**
This Kenyan SL28 coffee from the Karinga wet mill has a very distinct and intense fruity flavour with a refreshing acidity.
Expect winey notes of blackberries and rose hips with delicate floral aromas.
| **CULTIVAR**      | SL28 & SL34                      |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------- |
| **FLAVOUR NOTES** | Floral, blackberries & rose hips |
| **PRODUCER**      | Several smallholders farmers     |
| **COUNTRY**       | Kenya                            |
| **REGION**        | Kiambu                           |
| **PROCESS**       | Washed                           |
| **HARVEST**       | December 2020                    |
| **ROAST PROFILE** | Light Roast                      |
| **CONTENTS**      | Whole Coffee Beans               |
This coffee is grown at around 1900 masl in Kiambu where the climate is cool and makes the coffee ripen slower and develop more flavour, sweetness and acidity.
Most of the members who sell their coffee cherries to this wet mill grow mainly the **SL28** cultivar (with some growing **SL34**) because of good yields and exceptional quality in high altitudes. The majority of the farmers in this area have therefore been reluctant to plant the newer rust tolerant hybrids **Batian**and **Ruiru 11** which are becoming more common in lower altitudes in Kenya.
For me this coffee has been a standout for several years when I have been tasting coffees in Kenya because of its intensity of flavour. This year, I find the coffee to be quite winey and floral with intense blackberry and rose hip flavours. It is a very complex coffee that displays layers of flavour as it cools, so make sure you enjoy this coffee at various temperatures.
**FILTER**
****
We strongly recommend using the correct measurements and brewing techniques when you brew our coffees. Use a digital scale both to measure water and coffee in order to get consistent results, and we recommend using between 60 to 70 grams of coffee per litre (1000g) of water, depending on the brewing method, water quality and coffee used.
**ESPRESSO**
****
We strongly recommend using VST filter baskets. Both the 18g, 20g and the 22g basket is great for our coffee. The VST filters makes it a lot easier to extract the espresso properly which gives a lot more sweetness in the cup. They are also more or less identical to each other which makes it easy to be more consistent when brewing on several groups at the same time. You can buy the filters on our webshop, just make sure they fit your machine (ours fits all La Marzocco machines and machines with 58mm filter baskets). With the VST 18g filter basket, we recommend the following brewing parameters: 18-19g freshly-ground coffee, 25-35s brewing time, 35-38g of final brew liquid in the cup, 93°C-94°C brew water temperature.

beans.name: Honduras La Paz Caballero Catuai
beans.description:
**Caballero Catuai**
The majority of the **Caballero's** farms are planted with the Catuaí cultivar which used to be the most commonly grown
cultivar in their area. Expect a rich and chocolaty coffee with subtle flavours of dried fruits and apples with a herbal
finish.
| **CULTIVAR**      | Catuaí                               |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| **FLAVOUR NOTES** | Chocolate, dried fruits & apples     |
| **PRODUCER**      | Marysabel Caballero & Moises Herrera |
| **COUNTRY**       | Honduras                             |
| **REGION**        | Chinacla, La Paz                     |
| **PROCESS**       | Washed                               |
| **HARVEST**       | February 2021                        |
| **ROAST PROFILE** | Light Roast                          |
| **CONTENTS**      | Whole Coffee Beans                   |
| **BAG SIZE**      | 250g                                 |
Since we started buying coffees from Marysabel and Moises we have focused a lot on perfecting the process together in order to make their coffees taste clean, consistent and have a long shelf life.Two years ago, Moises started working a bit more on the fermentation stage when processing his Catuaí coffees. His wet mill is equipped with Penagos de-pulpers that have mechanical mucilage removers. This means that fermentation is not really necessary because most of the mucilage is removed from the beans mechanically, before they get dried. Still, Moises wanted to see if he could improve the quality slightly by letting the coffee ferment with the small amounts of mucilage still left on the coffee. The coffee is now fermented over night before washing and drying.
I must say that I have noticed more fruity flavours in the coffee in the last two years and also the quality in the last years have greatly improved. The coffees are sweeter, have more concentration of flavour and the best lots have a delicious dried fruit, plum and red apple like flavour to them.
Although Marysabel and Moises produce a lot of the Catuaí cultivar, not all lots are produced the same way. Some are dried on patios and others in mechanical driers or on raised beds under shade. Over the years I have developed a protocol in cooperation with Moises and Marysabel on how to separate, process and especially dry and store the coffees. In the last 5-6 years they have been producing a lot of Catuaí coffees especially for us to choose from.
All the lots we buy are dried on raised beds in shade and are hand sorted and milled with extreme care and attention. It really makes a difference in the final cup. In fact Moises was the one who let me experiment with shade dried coffee in the first place and we found that the green coffee will stay fresh for a longer time and taste much cleaner than the patio or mechanically dried coffees they produce.
**FILTER**
****
We strongly recommend using the correct measurements and brewing techniques when you brew our coffees. Use a digital scale both to measure water and coffee in order to get consistent results, and we recommend using between 60 to 70 grams of coffee per litre (1000g) of water, depending on the brewing method, water quality and coffee used.
**ESPRESSO**
****
We strongly recommend using VST filter baskets. Both the 18g, 20g and the 22g basket is great for our coffee. The VST filters makes it a lot easier to extract the espresso properly which gives a lot more sweetness in the cup. They are also more or less identical to each other which makes it easy to be more consistent when brewing on several groups at the same time. You can buy the filters on our webshop, just make sure they fit your machine (ours fits all La Marzocco machines and machines with 58mm filter baskets). With the VST 18g filter basket, we recommend the following brewing parameters: 18-19g freshly-ground coffee, 25-35s brewing time, 35-38g of final brew liquid in the cup, 93°C-94°C brew water temperature.

beans.name: Kenya Nyeri Karogoto
beans.description:
****
**Karogoto Espresso**
This Kenyan coffee from the **Karogoto wet mill,** has a very distinct and intense fruity flavour with a refreshing acidity. Expect winey notes of rose hips, hiciscus and blackcurrants.
| **CULTIVAR**      | SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11 & Batian       |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| **FLAVOUR NOTES** | Rose hips, hibiscus & blackcurrants |
| **PRODUCER**      | Several smallholders farmers        |
| **COUNTRY**       | Kenya                               |
| **REGION**        | Nyeri                               |
| **PROCESS**       | Washed                              |
| **HARVEST**       | December 2020                       |
| **ROAST PROFILE** | Espresso Roast                      |
| **CONTENTS**      | Whole Coffee Beans                  |
The coffees from Karogoto are always super clean and have an intense fruity and floral flavour. This is most likely because most of the members / farmers are still growing the SL28 cultivar and have received a lot of agricultural training over the years. The climatic conditions also contributes to the coffees intensity. The cool nights and moderately hot days slow down the ripening process and gives a very sweet, intense and unique coffee.
The 2020 harvest from Karogoto is very similar to the 2019 harvest with a vibrant acidity and a distinct flavour profile reminiscent of rose hips and hibiscus. The consistent quality of this coffee year after year shows that Karogoto is a very well managed wet mill much thanks to Ephraim, the wet-mill manager, who has been in charge of processing this coffee for many years. Ephraim has a great attention to details which I think contributes to this coffee’s remarkable cleanliness and refreshing acidity which are some of the attributes I love about this coffee.
**FILTER**
****
We strongly recommend using the correct measurements and brewing techniques when you brew our coffees. Use a digital scale both to measure water and coffee in order to get consistent results, and we recommend using between 60 to 70 grams of coffee per litre (1000g) of water, depending on the brewing method, water quality and coffee used.
**ESPRESSO**
****
We strongly recommend using VST filter baskets. Both the 18g, 20g and the 22g basket is great for our coffee. The VST filters makes it a lot easier to extract the espresso properly which gives a lot more sweetness in the cup. They are also more or less identical to each other which makes it easy to be more consistent when brewing on several groups at the same time. You can buy the filters on our webshop, just make sure they fit your machine (ours fits all La Marzocco machines and machines with 58mm filter baskets). With the VST 18g filter basket, we recommend the following brewing parameters: 18-19g freshly-ground coffee, 25-35s brewing time, 35-38g of final brew liquid in the cup, 93°C-94°C brew water temperature.

beans.description:
**GUATEMALA ROSMA**
**honeydew, clementine, caramel**
****
Location: Huehuetenango

beans.name: Ethiopia Gedeb Lot338 Landrace Natural Process

components.name: Gedeb Lot338 Landrace Natural Process
components.description: Ethiopia Gedeb Lot338 Landrace Natural Process

beans.description:
TASTING NOTES: Exotic | Blueberry, Raspberry, Malt Chocolate
ORIGIN: Yirgacheffe
PROCESS: Natural
VARIETAL: Heirlooms
ELEVATION: 1900m - 2200m
Suggested listening: Omar - There's Nothing Like This
Gedeb is a region in southern Ethiopia, best known to coffee buyers and drinkers as Yirgacheffe. It is perhaps the most famous growing region in all of the country. Prized for its heirloom coffees, exceptional coffee growing altitudes & soil, and of course the knowledge and experience of its ancient farmers.
​
Yirgacheffe and the heirloom varieties of coffees grown there have traditionally been seen as the California and Chardonnay of the coffee world. Bright & complex; floral, citrus and tea like flavors dominate the cup.  This is a nice analogy when discussing washed coffees from the Yirgacheffe region.
​
This however is a natural process coffee, meaning the fruit was dried inside the cherry. Therefore a better wine comparison for this would be more Malbec or Cabernet Sav than Chardonnay.
Deep and rich in body, intensely sweet dark and red berries dominate the flavor profile. The acidity whilst still pronounced is much heavier and less bright than a washed coffee from the same producing region.
​
In short Yirgacheffe/Gedeb Zone coffees are very wine-like, in so much as they are incredibly flavor complex. And at grade one level as this organic & natural offering is about as flavor complex as they come.
​
This particular coffee is sourced from METAD Agricultural Development PLC (METAD).
​
METAD is a third generation family owned business with a rich history that began after World War II when the Ethiopian Emperor awarded Muluemebet Emiru, the first African female pilot and family matriarch, with land in the Guji and Sidama zones that has become the Hambela Coffee Estate.
​
METAD is managed by Aman Adinew who returned to Ethiopia after many years working abroad at the executive level for multiple fortune 500 companies because he wanted to make a difference for his family and community. Through Aman’s leadership, METAD has strengthened the local community with employment opportunities including a workforce that is over seventy percent women, educational opportunities including sponsorship for a state-of-the-art elementary school with more than four hundred students, and healthcare for employees.
​
METAD was also the first to partner with Grounds for Health in Ethiopia to implement a successful cervical cancer screening program for women within the coffee growing communities. METAD provides technical assistance and shares modern farming equipment with other local farmers.  METAD also has the first and only private state-of-the-art SCAA certified coffee quality control lab on the African continent used to train both domestic and international coffee professionals.
​
A fruit bomb of real depth, sweetness and beauty.

beans.name: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe Welde Hirbe Field Blend Natural Process

components.name: Yirgacheffe Welde Hirbe Field Blend Natural Process
components.description: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe Welde Hirbe Field Blend Natural Process

beans.description:
TASTING NOTES: Exotic | Blueberry, Peaches, Cream
ORIGIN: Gedeo Zone, Yirgacheffe
PROCESS: Natural
VARIETAL: Landrace
ELEVATION: 1600-1800m
Suggested Listening: "La Ritournelle" Sebastien Teller
A microlot from Yirgacheffe that has that prized blueberry note and graded at the top of the scale so that there is very little funk to this coffee at all, its very, very clean with some nice florals dancing over the top of those super sweet berry notes.
This coffee is produced by Wolde Hirbe and processed as a separate micro lot at the Adame Gorbota Coop where Wolde is a member. This coop is located in the district of Wenago in the Gedeo Zone.
Wolde Hirbe is part of the single producer project developed jointly between the Yirgacheffe coffee farmers coop union and Royal Coffee Oakland. A project initiated in 2012 with a select group of model farmer/producers who have been willing to work within a rigorous set of processing standards to achieve higher cup qualifications. In exchange for agreeing to these standards the idea is for producers to achieve higher quality premiums for their lots.
Wolde with 30 years cultivating coffee under his belt across a 10 acre farm was selected for this project for his experience. His micro lot coffees like this one regularly achieve the grade 1 status that enables him to better support his family of 10.
This is a versatile coffee across a spectrum of brew methods, we suggest keeping the water temp high and letting rest for a minimum of 10 days after roast. After that the party is on....simply brew, play the suggested listening music and sit back and marvel at the wondrous beauty of coffee and music.
In Particular Ethiopian Coffee and bassline in music....some might say one of lifes great pairings.
​

beans.name: Juicebox Series – Karimikui AA
beans.description:
With sweet purple berry jam, pomegranate, vanilla, and ever more vibrant as the cup cools, this delightful mix of SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11, & Batian varieties are grown by smallholder farmers organized around Karimikui washing station as a part of Rungeto Cooperative in Kirinyaga, Kenya
​
​
​
Producers: smallholder farmers organized around Karimikui washing station
Origin: Kirinyaga County, Kenya
Varieties: SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11, Batian
Processing: Washed after depulping and fermenting, then soaked in clean water and dried on raised beds
Soil: Volcanic loam
Altitude: 1310-1900 masl
Harvest: Dec. 2020, outturn 13
Tasting notes: berries, pomegranate, spice cookies

beans.name: Hacienda Esmeralda Catuai Natural Process

components.name: Hacienda Esmeralda Catuai Natural Process
components.description: Panama Hacienda Esmeralda Catuai Natural Process

beans.description:
Suggested Listening: Jumbo - Underworld
The story of the Panamanian farm Hacienda Esmeralda and the now world famous Gesha varietal of coffee is one of hardship and brilliant triumph. In the 1990s, the Peterson family observed a patch of their farmland at Hacienda Esmeralda in Panama become afflicted with leaf rust, or “la roya” as its also called. A type of fungus that coats leaves and restricts the level of photosynthesis that can be achieved and results in a slow death of the plant.
In an effort to recoup the damages done to the bourbon/typica varietals initially lost to the fungus, the Petersons made a critical decision. They had noticed a spindly long stemmed tree that seemed to be able to withstand La Roya better than others. That tree was the Gesha varietal coffee plant and little was known about it back then other than it seemed strong and resilient. In the eleventh hour the Petersons decided to plant Gesha trees in the previously rust-stricken plots of land. This call to plant these ancient Ethiopian varietals in both the higher elevation and rust stricken zones of their farms coupled with the development of a brand new wet mill to enhance the processing of their coffees, resulted in a coffee that took the world by storm in the early 2000s. Prior to 2004 it was unheard of to hear of coffees exceeding $5 per pound green. At the best of Panama 2004 Esmeraldas washed Gesha sold for $21 per pound. 17 years on from that ground breaking price we are now seeing Geshas from Panama sell at an eye watering eyeing $2000 + per pound green.
Gesha has become the holy grail for coffee connoisseurs, and Panama its preferred country of origin for its growth. It is a unique country for growing coffee, a thin land mass enables it to catch two trade winds from two different ocean/seas. Aided by the rich, volcanic soil, the expertise of the Peterson family and their staff, the Gesha coffee varietal made a name for itself in the industry for unmatched flavor complexity.
Blossoming with intense, floral sweetness in both the nose and palate, the lushness of this cup is balanced elegantly with a distinct citrus pop of acidity. The rich florals come through tea-like and are followed by wonderfully sweet notes of ripe mango and papaya.  Despite this offering being a washed process bean the mouthfeel is luxurious. The whole experience of Gesha coffee is transcendental.
The pour over manual brew method is our suggested go to for this. The blooming of the grounds is worth this brew method choice alone as it unleashes an exquisite bouquet of Jasmine and dewy citrus blossom, the resulting brew an unrivaled balance of fleshy stone fruit sweetness and florality.
​
There is coffee. And then there is Gesha coffee. Jumbo levels of complexity.

beans.name: 과테말라 아구아 티비아 게이샤 워시드
beans.description:
가성비 좋은 게이샤 워시드를 소개합니다. 저는 원두를 테스팅 할 때 밤에 일과가 끝나고 조용한 시간에 집중하면서 테스트 노트를 적는데 이 친구는 새벽작업이 끝나고 여유 시간이 있어 테스팅을 진행했는데요, 특식치고는 밸런스가 꽤 좋고 꽉 찬 느낌이 마치 든든한 국밥 느낌을 받아 아침에 마시기 참 좋은 원두라는 생각이 들었습니다.
플로럴한 향미와 복숭아 단향 같은 분쇄향 감귤같은 상큼한 추출향이 좋습니다. 맛의 베이스는 레몬그라스이며 중간에 어떠한 느낌을 첨가한 향신료 톤도 살짝 올라옵니다.
뒷맛은 메이플 시럽 같은 향미와 단맛이 잡아주고 전체적인 밸런스를 유지해 준다는 느낌이 듭니다.
식었을 때는 밀크초콜릿이 툭 튀어나오는데, 인위적인 로스팅에 의한 밀크 초콜릿과 다른 생초콜릿과 같은 느낌이 들어 더욱 특별했습니다.
요즘같은 추워진 날씨에 따듯하게 드시기 더 할 나위 없는 원두라 생각합니다.

beans.description:
재미있는 특식 원두를 소개해 드립니다. 가향 커피 입니다. 펄프를 벗긴 커피 체리에 타르타르산과 시나몬을 넣어 72시간 동안 드라이 무산호 발효한 뒤 시나몬과 함께 돋음건조대에서 말렸습니다.
프로듀서 하이로 아르실라가 생산한 커피는 저희가 2019년부터 꾸준히 판매하고 있는데요, 너무 인기가 좋아 늘 120~180Kg씩 판매했던 기록이 있습니다. 이번에 소개하는 커피는 그 커피들 중 가장 환성도가 높다고 생각하는 커피입니다.
저는 이 커피를 테스팅 하고 그라인딩 하고 추출하는 순간 딱 세가지의 커핑 노트가 떠올랐습니다. 시나몬롤, 애플파이, 짱구과자인데요, 몇년 전 유명한 빵집에서 시나몬롤을 처음 사먹었을 때 이과 같은 달콤한 향이 나와 너무 맛있게 먹었는데 그 향이 이 커피에서 나와서 깜짝 놀랐습니다.
전체적으로 시나몬 이라는 캐릭터가 향미, 맛에서 모두 함께 합니다.
추출향에서는 몇달 전 너무 인기가 많아 생두사에 있는 모든 생두를 구입했던 코스타리카 산 가브리엘 무산소 허니와 비슷한 짱구과자 향이 납니다. 정말 재미납니다.
맛에서는 애플파이를 한 입 베어물었을 때 나오는 달콤하면서 산ㄴ뜻한 산미와 단맛이 너무 좋습니다. 입에 쩍쩍 달라붙는 단맛이 쭉 이어집니다. 핑크버번종이 가지고 있는 단맛과 시나몬이라는 가향이 적절하게 섞인 느낌입니다.
가향커피라는 편견을 버리면 이런 가격에 이런 커피를 접할 수 있을까? 라는 생각을 합니다.
아마 가격만큼 고객님들께 새로운 경험을 드리지 않을까 싶습니다.

beans.name: 에티오피아 '아돌라' G1 내츄럴
beans.description:
# 에티오피아 구지 '아돌라' G1 내츄럴, 스페셜리저브
## Ethiopia Guji 'Adola' G1 Natural, Special Reserve
> **New Crop 100%, Specialty Coffee Bean**
★ 생두 직수입과 다국적 기업 협력을 통하여 좋은 품질의 원두와 생두를 합리적인 가격에 제공하고 있습니다.
★ 당해 생산된 최고급 품질의 생두만 선별하여 직접 로스팅하고 있습니다.
★ 원두의 경우 로스팅전후로 2번의 핸드픽을 통하여 꼼꼼하게 결점두를 제거하여 맛의 균일성을 유지하고 있습니다.
★ 당사에서는 원두 본연의 향미를 위해 인위적인 다크로스팅이나 강배전 로스팅은 지양합니다.
★ 신선함과 품질은 높이고 가격은 낮추었습니다.
> *오로미아 지역에서 Guji는 시다모와 예가체프와 근접한 SNNPR(남방 민족지역)의 국경을 가로질러 있는 곳에 위치해 있습니다. 이 커피는 2014년 설립된 패밀리 소유 사업체인 시부 커피를 통해 제공되는 것으로 중앙 경매를 위한 커피 공급 업무를 20년 넘게 진행해온 경력이 있으며 Karcha Guracho와 Hambala Damedabaye지역에 총 3개의 워싱 스테이션을 소유하고 있습니다.*
>
> *시부커피는 작물의 지속가능성을 향상시키는 것에 초점을 맞추고 보육원과 각종 서비스를 바탕으로 양질의 종자 선택, 파종-수확-수확 후 관리 전반에 걸쳐 농부들과 직접적으로 협력하고 있습니다.*
>
> *시부커피는 특히 자체 농장을 소유하고 있으며 이 농장에는 약 3000명의 농부들과 함께 일하고 있습니다. 구지 지역의 커피는 영양분이 풍부한 토양 및 Shade Grown으로 재배되고 있습니다. 구지 커피는 유기농 인증을 받지 않았지만 화학비료가 전혀 사용되지 않아 시부가 말하는 자연 유기농 상태의 커피가 제공되고 있습니다.*
***Origin Info***

beans.name: 콜롬비아 안티오키아 부에노스 아이레스 워시드
beans.description:
이번에 소개해드리는 원두는 위 설명 처럼 관리가 잘 된 생두 입니다. 깨끗하며 커핑 노트가 복합적이고 개성이 있었습니다.
다양한 얼굴을 가진 캐릭터 같습니다. 맛에서는 누가! 자두! 시럽! 이렇게 세가지가 잘 느껴지는데, 향미에서는 네츄럴 같은 플로럴하면서 보라색 꽃 같은 느낌이 들었습니다.
향미와 맛이 따로 느껴져 아마 추출하는 환경과 조건에 따라 여러 방향으로 갈릴 것 같음을 조심스레 생각해봅니다. 후미에 묵직한 시럽 느낌과 초콜릿 후미가 함께 들어와 이 부분은 고객분들에게 편하게 다가갈 듯 합니다.
산미가 크게 튀지 않고 식었을 때도 캬라멜톤의 단맛이 받쳐주어 '처음부터 끝까지 개성과 기본기를 두루 갖춘 좋은 생두다'라는 생각이 들었습니다.
가성비 좋으며 향미와 베이스가 탄탄한 아이이며 많은 분들이 좋아해 주셨으면 합니다.

beans.name: 에티오피아 리무 자페르 내추럴
beans.description:
겨울이 다가 올 수록 좋은 에티오피아를 찾기 힘들다는 것은 커피를 즐기시는 분들이라면 이해하실 듯 합니다. 커피 보릿고개라는 겨울이 시작되면 저 또한 좋은 생둘르 찾기 위해 많은 고민을 하는데요, 이러한 고민을 잡아준 고마운 생두가 있어 여러분께 소개시켜 드립니다.
너무 마음에 들어 120Kg덥썩 구매한 친구입니다. 과일과일함이 가득하고 진득함이 가득한 원두입니다.
플로럴한 휼빈 다음 딸기 크림같은 부드러운 분쇄향, 열대과일 톤의 추출향과 달달하고 복숭복숭한 시음 전 향 등 향미 부분에서 강하게 이펙트 있다고 느꼈습니다.
맛에서는 청포도 톤이 베이스이며 입안에 커피를 굴렸을 때 청포도를 한 움쿰 집어 먹은 듯한 질감과 부드러움이 매력 입니다. 좋은 질감 뒤에 오는 단맛은 마치 시럽과 같으면서 진득한 느낌입니다.
입안에 달라붙는 시러피함은 상큼 달달한 과일차를 먹었을 때 나오는 느낌과 비슷합니다.
단맛과 산미가 가격대비 뛰어나며 가성비 좋은 아이로 많은 분들께 사랑받을 에티오피아 원두가 될 것 같습니다.

beans.name: 콜롬비아 페레즈
beans.description:
**크리스마스를 위한 특별한 커피**
매년 크리스마스마다 커피콜렉티브에서는 특별한 커피를 선보이죠.
완벽한 크리스마스를 위해 커피콜렉티브가 선택한 커피를 프리오더로 만나보세요.
------
**콜롬비아 페레즈**
**JULEKAFFE PEREZ**
TOLIMA, WASHED
**TASTING NOTE**
균형잡힌 단맛, 바디감과 함께 섬세한 산미가 느껴지는 커피.
오렌지필, 머스코바도 설탕과 만다린의 복합적인 아로마를 느껴보세요.
**ABOUT THIS COFFEE**
커피콜렉티브에서는 매년 크리스마스마다 이 특별한 축제 시즌을 기념하는 새로운 커피를 선보여요. 그리고 올해에도 크리스마스에 완벽하게 어울리는 커피를 준비했습니다.
크리스마스 시즌을 위해 커피콜렉티브는 레이넬 페레즈가 생산한 커피 중에서도 은은한 과일의 뉘앙스로 함께 하는 크리스마스에 즐기기 좋은 특별한 랏의 커피를 선택했어요.
소중한 사람들과 함께 나누는 한잔의 커피가 축제의 아침에 한 조각의 작은 즐거움을 가져다줄 수 있길 바랄게요.
Julekaffe Perez가 생산된 Finca circasia의 풍경
커핑을 하고있는 생산자 Reinel Perez
**FACTSHEET**
**생산지 |** 콜롬비아 (Colombia)
**생산자 |** Reinel Perez Ospina
**지역 |** 톨리마 (Tolima)
**품종 |** Caturra, Colombia, Typica
**고도 |** 1,500 masl
**수확기 |** June
**프로세스 |** 워시드 (Washed)
------
**PACKAGE**
유럽(네덜란드)에서 생산된 패키지로, 원재료의 50%가 재활용된 플라스틱이며 100% 재활용 가능한 소재입니다.

beans.name: 니카라과 리틀 레드 라이딩 후드
beans.description:
**Little Red Riding Hood**
원산지 : 니카라과 ( Nicaragua )
지역 : 누에바 세고비아, 디삘또, 라스 마노스 ( Las Manos, Dipilto, Nueva Segovia )
재배고도 : 1,350 ~ 1700m
농장명 : 로스 피리네오스 ( Los Pirineos ) 외 다수 농장
품종 : 카투라 ( Caturra )
가공방식 : Little Red Riding Hood ( CM Natural )
로스팅 :  미디엄 라이트 ( Medium Light )
용량 : 200g
Cup note : White Wine, Prune, Cinnamon
화이트 와인, 프룬, 시나몬
**+ Little Red Riding Hood**
**신선한 과일향을 표현하기 위한 CM ( 카보닉 마세레이션 ) 프로파일로 짧은 시간 발효를 시켰습니다.**
**발효를 균일하게 통제하기 위해 작은 배치사이즈로 발효를 진행했고 이후 체리를 꺼내**
**아프리칸 베드에 얇게 펴서 건조 후 다시 아프리칸 베드에 체리를 쌓아 건조를 진행하였습니다.**
****
매일 매일 다양한 커피를 테이스팅 하지만,
여러분들에게 소개해드릴 커피는 항상 많은 고민을 하게 되는듯 해요
이 커피를 처음 마셨을때 화이트 와인 혹은 샴페인을 마실때와 같은 느낌을 받아 무척 매력적이게 다가왔어요
곧 다가올 연말에 잘 어울릴 것 같아 이 커피를 선택하게 되었어요
니카라과 Little Red Riding Hood 는 와인같은 새콤 달콤한 산미를 느껴보고 싶으신 분에게 추천드려요
커피를 분쇄하고 내리는 동안 청포도와 같은 향기와 함께
화이트 와인 같은 아로마를 시작으로 프룬 플레이버, 천도 복숭아와 같은 산미, 마시고 난 후엔 시나몬의 단맛과 향이 지속됩니다.
**· Recipe : 룰커피에서는 40 - 60 - 60 - 60 으로 제공해 드리고 있습니다.**
****
**· TIP : 좀 더 와이니한 느낌을 받고 싶으시면 콜드브루로 만들어 드시면 아주 매력적일거라 생각합니다**

beans.name: Honduras La Paz Caballero Catuai Washed

components.name: La Paz Caballero Catuai Washed
components.description: Honduras La Paz Caballero Catuai Washed

beans.description:
**Caballero Catuai**
The majority of the **Caballero's** farms are planted with the Catuaí cultivar which used to be the most commonly grown
cultivar in their area. Expect a rich and chocolaty coffee with subtle flavours of dried fruits and apples with a herbal
finish.
| **CULTIVAR**      | Catuaí                               |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| **FLAVOUR NOTES** | Chocolate, dried fruits & apples     |
| **PRODUCER**      | Marysabel Caballero & Moises Herrera |
| **COUNTRY**       | Honduras                             |
| **REGION**        | Chinacla, La Paz                     |
| **PROCESS**       | Washed                               |
| **HARVEST**       | February 2021                        |
| **ROAST PROFILE** | Light Roast                          |
| **CONTENTS**      | Whole Coffee Beans                   |
| **BAG SIZE**      | 250g                                 |
Since we started buying coffees from Marysabel and Moises we have focused a lot on perfecting the process together in order to make their coffees taste clean, consistent and have a long shelf life.Two years ago, Moises started working a bit more on the fermentation stage when processing his Catuaí coffees. His wet mill is equipped with Penagos de-pulpers that have mechanical mucilage removers. This means that fermentation is not really necessary because most of the mucilage is removed from the beans mechanically, before they get dried. Still, Moises wanted to see if he could improve the quality slightly by letting the coffee ferment with the small amounts of mucilage still left on the coffee. The coffee is now fermented over night before washing and drying.
I must say that I have noticed more fruity flavours in the coffee in the last two years and also the quality in the last years have greatly improved. The coffees are sweeter, have more concentration of flavour and the best lots have a delicious dried fruit, plum and red apple like flavour to them.
Although Marysabel and Moises produce a lot of the Catuaí cultivar, not all lots are produced the same way. Some are dried on patios and others in mechanical driers or on raised beds under shade. Over the years I have developed a protocol in cooperation with Moises and Marysabel on how to separate, process and especially dry and store the coffees. In the last 5-6 years they have been producing a lot of Catuaí coffees especially for us to choose from.
All the lots we buy are dried on raised beds in shade and are hand sorted and milled with extreme care and attention. It really makes a difference in the final cup. In fact Moises was the one who let me experiment with shade dried coffee in the first place and we found that the green coffee will stay fresh for a longer time and taste much cleaner than the patio or mechanically dried coffees they produce.
**FILTER**
****
We strongly recommend using the correct measurements and brewing techniques when you brew our coffees. Use a digital scale both to measure water and coffee in order to get consistent results, and we recommend using between 60 to 70 grams of coffee per litre (1000g) of water, depending on the brewing method, water quality and coffee used.
**ESPRESSO**
****
We strongly recommend using VST filter baskets. Both the 18g, 20g and the 22g basket is great for our coffee. The VST filters makes it a lot easier to extract the espresso properly which gives a lot more sweetness in the cup. They are also more or less identical to each other which makes it easy to be more consistent when brewing on several groups at the same time. You can buy the filters on our webshop, just make sure they fit your machine (ours fits all La Marzocco machines and machines with 58mm filter baskets). With the VST 18g filter basket, we recommend the following brewing parameters: 18-19g freshly-ground coffee, 25-35s brewing time, 35-38g of final brew liquid in the cup, 93°C-94°C brew water temperature.

beans.name: 아크멜 스페셜
beans.description:
**에티오피아 아크멜 스페셜**
**ETHIOPIA AKMEL SPECIAL**
Jimma, Anaerobic Natural
**TASTING NOTE**
단맛과 산미의 적절한 밸런스로 강렬한 맛을 느낄 수 있는 커피입니다.
초콜렛과 향신료, 딸기와 파파야의 향을 느껴보세요.
**ABOUT THIS COFFEE**
커피콜렉티브는 2014년부터 에티오피아의 커피 생산자, 아크멜의 커피를 꾸준히 소개하며 흥미로운 프로젝트들을 진행해왔어요. 이 커피도 그 프로젝트의 일부로, 무산소 발효공법 Anaerobic이라 불리는 실험적인 공법으로 탄생했습니다.
무산소 발효공법은 밀폐된 통에 압력을 가해 커피체리의 과즙을 커피콩 안에 응축시키고, 그 과정에서 독특한 컵 프로파일을 만들어냅니다.
울창한 야생의 숲속, 높은 나무 그림자 아래서 익어가며 더욱 응축된 맛을 지니게 된 아크멜의 커피를 화려하고 야성적이며 쥬시하게 풀어낸 아크멜 스페셜을 지금 만나보세요.
내추럴 가공방식으로 가공된 후 건조중인 커피체리들
시험 발효중인 커피체리
농장 경영에 관해 이야기하고있는 아크멜 누리(Akmel Nuri)
**FACTSHEET**
**생산지 |** 에티오피아 (Ethiopia)
**지역 |** 짐마 (Jimma)
**생산자 |** 아크멜 누리 (Akmel Nuri)
**품종 |** 토착종 (Heirloom)
**고도 |** 1,800 - 2,200 masl
**수확기 |** Nobember - January
**프로세스 |** 무산소발효 내추럴 (Anaerobic Natural)

beans.name: 과테말라 엘 인헤르또 파카마라 워시드
beans.description:
> *과테말라 엘 인헤르또 파카마라 풀리 워시드*
>
> **
>
> *[중약배전]*
>
> *디개싱 최소 7 ~ 10일*
>
> *소비기한  4 ~ 6주 이내를 권장합니다.*
> ***재스민, 핵과, 오렌지 선명한 인탠스와 클린 한 피니시 달콤한 후미***
>
> ******
>
> ***네임드 농장인 엘 인헤르또 에서 출하한 파카마라 품종입니다.***
>
> ***재스민과 핵과류의 향미가 느껴지며 오렌지스러운 끈적한 단맛과 산미톤이 인상적입니다.***
>
> ***파카마라 품종에서 오는 선명한 인탠스와 클린 컵 그리고 다 마시고 올라오는***
>
> ***하얀 꽃향기와 달콤한 향기는 풍성한 한 잔을 완성 시켜 줍니다.***
>
> ******
>
> ***핫, 아이스 모두 권장 드립니다.***
>
> ****충분히 디개싱 한 후 드시면 더욱 맛있습니다.***

beans.name: 콜롬비아 포토시 트레스 드래곤즈 내추럴
beans.description:
21년도 1월 경 테이스팅을 하여 과하지 않은 화사한 향미들이 정말 마음에 들어서 판매를 했었던 커피 입니다. 이번에 재입고 되었다는 반가운 소식을 듣고, 정말 좋았었던 기억에 빠르게 재 구매하여 다시 한번 더 소개 해드릴 수 있게 되었습니다.
이 커피는 내추럴 가공이지만 무산소 발효로 가공을 한 커피처럼 매우 밝고 화사한 특징들이 돋보이는 커피입니다.
이번에 다시 테이스팅을 하면서도 1월초에 소개해드렸을 떄 포토시의 밝은 향미가 잘 유지되고 있었네요. 전체적으로 이 커피에서 핑크색이 많이 연상 되었습니다.
복합적인 향미가 뛰어나며 클린함이 좋은 포토시 내추럴

beans.name: 콜롬비아 엘파라이소 무산소발효 로즈티
beans.description:
이번에 입고된 커피는 엘 파라이소 농장의 이중 무산소 발효로 가공된 '로즈티'라는 커피 입니다. 분쇄할때와 아로마에서는 엘파라이소 리치와 흡사한 복숭아, 리치 같은 향이 느껴집니다.
이 커피의 가장 큰 특징은 밝은 산미 인 것 같습니다. 체리 히비스커스 티와 같은 상큼하고 밝은 향미가 첫모금부터 커피를 다 마실 때까지 선명하게 유지되는 것이 인상적이었습니다.
과하지 않은 깨끗한 느낌에 끌리게 되었고, 한마디로 표현하면 편하게 상큼한 과일쥬스를 마시는 느낌 같다고 느껴졌습니다.
질감은 실키하고 아주 부드러운 느낌입니다. 식고나서는 미미한 얼그레이 같은 느낌이 있고, 마시고 나서는 애프터에 밝은 산미의 과일쥬스의 느낌이 오랫동안 지속되는 점이 인상적입니다.

beans.name: 에티오피아 아바야 게이샤 내추럴 스트로베리
beans.description:
커피의 네이밍에 붙은 '스트로베리'라는 네이밍에 잘 맞게 스트로베리의 상큼한 밝은 산미가 매력적인 커피 입니다. 딸기의 향보다는 맛에서 많이 공감이 되었습니다. 내추럴의 복합적인 느낌보다는 선명하고 청량하다는 표현이 적절할 것 같습니다.
과일쥬스 딸기 같은 느낌의 특징이 요즘 에티오피아 커피를 많이 테스트 하였는데 비교적으로 선명하다고 느껴졌습니다. 밝은 산미에 이어지는 단맛은 백도복숭아처럼 느껴졌으며, 과일의 산미와 단맛의 하모니가 아주 좋은 커피 인 것 같습니다.
식을수록 더 밝아지고 상큼해지는 커피입니다. 마시고 나서 애프터에는 상큼한 과일 쥬스를 마신 듯한 밝은 산미가 오랫동안 남고, 클린하고 청량하게 마무리 됩니다.

beans.name: 후르츠 캔디
beans.description:
**★** **크리스마스 한정 블랜드입니다. 여러가지 과일 맛 사탕을 커피에서 느껴보세요.**
**★ 5개 대륙의 내추럴, 워시드 커피와 (게이샤를 포함한 각종 품종을 최적으로 조합.)**
**★ 모노스코프 다크 - 홀빈 아그트론 : 72~77,** **Medium Roast**
**★ 디개싱은 약 3-5일 권장합니다.**
**★ 모든 추출 레시피에 잘 나오도록 로스팅 했습니다.**
**★ 받으시는 커피는 전일 혹은 주문 당일 로스팅 제품을 발송합니다.**
**★ 원두(홀빈) 상태로 패킹 되기 때문에 분쇄 요청 시 상단면 개봉 흔적이 있습니다.**

beans.description:
색다른 발효 가공으로 우리를 즐겁게 해주었던 하이로 아르실라가 이번에는 발효한 생두를 얼렸다 말린 특이한 커피를 개발했다.
카스티요 품종 커피 체리를 24시간 드라이 무산소 발효--> 그레인프로 자루에 넣어 밀봉한 다음 섭씨 22도 미만 물탱크에 60 시간 동안 담금 --> 다시 72 시간 동안 냉동 -->섭씨 35도 미만을 유지하면서 돋움건조대에 말리는 과정을 거친 것.
결과는 딸기와 열대과일, 초콜렛의 단맛과 카카오의 쌉싸름함이 어우러진 맛난 커피!

24시간 동안 건식 무산소 발효한 다음 이산화탄소를 더해 그레인프로 자루에 담아 섭씨 22도 미만에서 다시 24시간 발효한 게샤 커피.
o마세라시옹
와인용어로 “포도 알을 으깨지 않은 상태에서 발효탱크에 이산화탄소를 넣고 발효시키는 것으로 색이 밝고, 과일 맛이 나며, 타닌(Tannin)의 양이 적게 나온다. 보졸레 와인(Beaujolais Wine) 포도주를 만드는 데 주로 쓰는 방법이다.

beans.description:
하이로는 1987년 그의 첫 농장인 라 에스메랄다 농장을 구입해서 카투라를 심었다. 그는 지역 수출업체에서 풀타임으로 일하면서 커피를 재배했고, 그렇게 번 돈으로 농장 다섯 개를 차근차근 구입했다. 비야라소로 시작해서 마사틀란, 산타모니카, 마라카이, 그리고 부에노스 아이레스까지. 그는 바로 코피넷 설립자의 아버지로, 커피 체리의 수확, 분류, 가공에 대한 아들의 전문성에 귀 기울여 왔다. 새로운 기술을 배워야 하고, 계속 새로운 시도를 하는 과정이 힘들었지만, 그가 재배한 게샤가 2019년 콜롬비아 컵오브엑설런스 대회에서 우승을 하게 되었다.
. 이 커피는 100% 카스티요 품종이다. 국립커피협회 Federacion Nacional de Cafeteros 가 개발한 이 품종은 카투라보다 생산량이 많고 커피잎 녹병에도 더 강하다. 말린 귤껍질을 넣어 그레인프로 자루에 담아 플라스틱 표준 탱크 안에서 48시간 동안 드라이 무산소 발효한 다음 섭씨 35도 미만으로 조절하며 알맞은 수분도에 이를 때까지 돋움건조대에서 말린 귤껍질과 같이 말려 향미를 더한다. 이 발효에 사용된 귤껍질도 자체 농장에서 생산한 것이다.

beans.name: 윈터
beans.description:
모모스시즈널 블렌드, 윈터가 출시되었습니다.
윈터는 에티오피아의 내추럴, 워시드 가공된 커피의 블렌딩으로 매년 많은 사랑을 받고 있습니다.
에티오피아 내추럴 커피에서 느껴지는 베리류의 달콤산뜻한 향미와 카라멜의 부드러운 단맛, 워시드 커피에서 느낄 수 있는 플로럴하면서 우아한 산미가 조화롭습니다. 일반적인 싱글 에스테이트 커피보다 살짝 배전도를 높여 필터 커피에서는 에티오피아 커피의 풍성한 향미를, 에스프레소 혹은 아메리카노에서는 기존 블렌드와는 또다른 느낌의 개성있는 커피를 만나실 수 있습니다. 밀도 높은 달콤함은 라떼나 카푸치노로 드셨을 때 더욱 배가되어 크림치즈 같은 묵직한 질감이 단맛을 더욱 상승시켜줍니다.
**NOTE**
라즈베리, 얼그레이, 카라멜, 시러피
**INFO**
Ethiopia Yirgacheffe Chelbesa Washed 40%
Ethiopia Hambela Buku Sayisa Natural 60%
**ROASTED COLOR #51 (Agtron)**
**ESPRESSO GUIDE**
VST 20g 바스켓
원두 21g 물 92℃ 24초 42g 추출
NOTE Raspyberry, Earl grey, Caramel
**BREWING GUIDE**
칼리타 웨이브 185 드리퍼
원두 20g 분쇄 물 90 ℃ 280g 주입 추출시간 총 2분 40초
40g 주입 블루밍 30초 - 80g 1차주입 - 80g 2차주입 - 80g 3차주입
(누적무게 40g - 120g - 200g - 280g)
